args.query: site:amazon.com/gp/help delivered package not received contact seller marketplace seller fulfilled A-to-z Guarantee

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Find a Missing Package That Shows As Delivered

  - url: https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=GCU8BWGTQNJKQEBS

  - source: Amazon Customer Service

  - domain: amazon.com


  ## Content


  FIND A MISSING PACKAGE THAT SHOWS AS DELIVERED


  When tracking shows that a package was delivered but the package cannot be found,
  review the delivery information before opening a new support request.


  1. Confirm the shipping address in Your Orders. Verify that the address shown for
  the affected order is correct.


  2. Look for a delivery-attempt notice or delivery confirmation. Check the Message
  Center, the order''s tracking details, the mailbox, and any other location where
  delivery notices are normally received. Review any available delivery photo.


  3. Check around the delivery location. Review the delivery instructions saved in
  Your Addresses and inspect any requested safe place, porch, reception area, parcel
  locker, garage, side entrance, or other nearby location where the package could
  have been left.


  4. Ask household members and neighbors whether someone else accepted the package.


  5. Allow up to 48 hours after the delivery scan. A package can sometimes be marked
  as delivered before it physically arrives.


  6. Check with the carrier when additional location information may be available.
  The carrier''s tracking page or contact information may provide more detail about
  the final scan.


  These checks are intended to help determine whether the package is delayed, was
  accepted by another person, or was left at a different delivery location. The page''s
  troubleshooting steps do not by themselves identify whether Amazon or a third-party
  seller is responsible for the affected order.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Find a Missing Package That Shows As Delivered
url: https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=GCU8BWGTQNJKQEBS

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Contact a Third-Party Seller - Amazon Customer Service

  - url: https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=GLC8ZMBWMBTR6QZZ

  - source: Amazon Customer Service

  - domain: amazon.com


  ## Content


  CONTACT A THIRD-PARTY SELLER


  Amazon provides separate methods for contacting a third-party seller before and
  after an order is placed.


  To contact a seller before ordering, open the product detail page and select the
  seller''s name. On the seller page, choose the option to ask a question, select
  the relevant subject, and send the message through Amazon''s messaging flow.


  To contact a seller after ordering:


  1. Go to Your Orders.

  2. Find the affected item or order.

  3. Select the option to contact the seller or report a problem with the order.

  4. Choose the relevant topic.

  5. Use the Seller Messaging Assistant or buyer-seller messaging flow to send the
  message.


  Seller correspondence can be reviewed in the Message Center under the buyer-seller
  messages area. The seller is generally expected to respond within 48 hours.


  This page explains how the seller-contact channel works. The availability of a contact
  option does not establish that the seller is the responsible first destination for
  every order involving a third-party seller. The order''s fulfillment and service
  relationship must still be checked separately.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Contact a Third-Party Seller - Amazon Customer Service
url: https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=GLC8ZMBWMBTR6QZZ

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: A-to-z Guarantee - Amazon Customer Service

  - url: https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=GQ37ZCNECJKTFYQV

  - source: Amazon Customer Service

  - domain: amazon.com


  ## Content


  A-TO-Z GUARANTEE


  The A-to-z Guarantee applies to purchases that are both sold and fulfilled by a
  third-party Marketplace seller that provides customer service directly to the buyer.
  It covers issues involving timely delivery and the condition of the item.


  A customer may be able to request an A-to-z Guarantee refund when the order was
  placed with a Marketplace seller, the last possible delivery date falls within the
  stated eligibility period, and at least one qualifying delivery or item-condition
  issue applies.


  For a package-not-received issue, the delivery conditions include a package that
  has not arrived after the applicable delivery timing threshold or tracking that
  shows delivery confirmation. The customer must also have contacted the seller and
  allowed the seller time to respond or resolve the issue before the guarantee request
  becomes eligible.


  Other covered situations can include an item that is damaged, defective, or not
  as described; a return that the seller did not properly authorize; certain international-return
  problems; or an expected refund that was not received after a qualifying return.


  The guarantee is a Marketplace protection route for seller-fulfilled transactions.
  It is not the standard customer-service path for every item sold by a third-party
  seller. The fulfillment relationship matters: the page describes orders where the
  third-party seller both sells and fulfills the item and directly provides customer
  service.

  '
metadata: {}
title: A-to-z Guarantee - Amazon Customer Service
url: https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=GQ37ZCNECJKTFYQV

content.content:
I checked order **ORD-AMZ-241107**:

- **Item:** LumaNest Rechargeable Closet Light Set  
- **Seller:** Arcadia Home Market, a third-party marketplace seller  
- **Fulfillment:** **Seller-fulfilled** (Seller-Fulfilled Prime), shipped by Arcadia Home Market  
- **Carrier status:** UPS marked it delivered on **July 9 at 20:14 UTC**  
- **Current status:** No seller contact or Amazon case has been opened

### Where to handle it

Start with **Amazon → Your Orders → ORD-AMZ-241107 → Contact seller / Report a problem with order**. Tell Arcadia Home Market that the package is marked delivered but missing. Amazon says seller correspondence is handled through its buyer-seller messaging system, and sellers generally have 48 hours to respond.

Before or while contacting the seller, check the delivery photo/details, nearby safe locations, mailbox, reception or lockers, household members, and neighbors. Amazon also recommends allowing up to **48 hours** after the delivery scan and checking with UPS for additional location details.

If the seller does not resolve it after you contact them and allow time to respond, use the **Amazon A-to-z Guarantee** route for a seller-fulfilled marketplace order.

- [Amazon: Find a Missing Package That Shows As Delivered](https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=GCU8BWGTQNJKQEBS)
- [Amazon: Contact a Third-Party Seller](https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=GLC8ZMBWMBTR6QZZ)
- [Amazon: A-to-z Guarantee](https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=GQ37ZCNECJKTFYQV)
